name: April 13 — The game of work
notes: Chase's real writing. Tests lowercase god/capital one, 'pipped' (not 'PIP'd'), M-F, 175k, 'Lets' without apostrophe, second-person self-address.

sections.heading: What Happened
sections.rawText:
You may think it's stupid to dress nicely and you may see through it when others dress nicely, but if you are the best dressed in the room and well groomed, it comes with power. Conversely, if you are poorly dressed, you will feel less powerful and people may treat you poorly. For the 3 days of the week you are in office, consider dressing in no less than a polo, but go for a nice button down shirt most days
Things you will be evaluated on:
- Productivity & Visible Impact: It doesn't just matter what you do, but how much business impact the work you did has
- Appearance
Dress sharp. 
Button down shirt

Steps to break out of the system, or at least try my best to, while maintaining focus on what matters most, connecting with god and accomplishing something with him

Figure out how capital one tracks productivity. Capital one has been known to lay off the bottom 15% of workers semi-annually. You get pipped and then fired. Make sure you stay above that threshold at a minimum. 
M-F at minimum you work 12 hour days. Get what you need to done at Capital One and then work for yourself, just as hard.

sections.heading: What I Learned
sections.rawText:
Justification: 
With a salary of 175k, you are in the top 1% of the global population. You should fight to maintain that
For a HOUSEHOLD in the US, you are in the top 10-15% of earners. You should fight hard to protect that . Lets go ahead and do A
